data.title: Aquestive Therapeutics (AQST) Q4 Loss Surge Tests Bullish Margin Expansion Narrative
data.description:
Aquestive Therapeutics (AQST) just wrapped up FY 2025 with Q4 total revenue of US$13.0 million and a basic EPS loss of US$0.26, alongside a trailing twelve month EPS loss of US$0.78 on US$44.5 million of revenue. The company has seen quarterly revenue move from US$8.7 million and a basic EPS loss of US$0.24 in Q1 2025 to US$10.0 million with a US$0.14 EPS loss in Q2, US$12.8 million with a US$0.14 EPS loss in Q3, and then US$13.0 million with a US$0.26 EPS loss in Q4. This leaves investors weighing top line momentum against pressure on margins and the pace of loss reduction.

See our full analysis for Aquestive Therapeutics.

With the latest report on the table, the next step is to see how these numbers stack up against the prevailing stories around Aquestive Therapeutics and which of those narratives hold up under the data.

See what the community is saying about Aquestive Therapeutics

US$44.5m in TTM revenue versus US$83.8m in losses

  • Over the trailing twelve months, Aquestive booked US$44.5 million of revenue alongside a net loss of US$83.8 million and a basic EPS loss of US$0.78, so every dollar of sales is still matched by almost US$2 of loss.
  • Bulls point to the potential for Anaphylm and the broader PharmFilm portfolio to drive higher operating leverage over time, yet the current loss profile means that
    • the bullish scenario of margins rising from about a 147.4% loss to a 4.1% profit by around 2028 starts from a base where FY 2025 net income remains deeply negative at US$31.9 million in Q4 alone, and
    • the idea of long term margin expansion has to be weighed against the trailing cash burn implied by US$83.8 million of losses on a relatively modest revenue base.

Bulls argue that recent revenue traction could be the early step toward the kind of earnings swing their forecasts assume, and that is exactly the tension the detailed bull case tries to unpack in more depth. 🐂 Aquestive Therapeutics Bull Case

Premium 11.7x P/S with negative equity

  • Shares change hands at an 11.7x P/S ratio, compared with 4.3x for the US pharmaceuticals industry and 6.5x for peers, while the company also reports negative shareholders' equity over the trailing twelve months.
  • Bears highlight that paying a premium multiple for a business that is still loss making and carrying negative equity leaves little room for setbacks, especially when
    • the company is forecast to remain unprofitable for at least the next three years despite revenue being forecast to grow at about 30.4% per year, and
    • recent shareholder dilution and a highly volatile share price add extra financing and market risk on top of that rich 11.7x P/S starting point.

Skeptics warn that when a US$4.26 stock already prices in a premium P/S and balance sheet pressure, any wobble in execution can matter more than usual, which is exactly what the detailed bear case lays out numerically. 🐻 Aquestive Therapeutics Bear Case

Quarterly losses widened again in Q4 2025

  • Q4 2025 net loss of US$31.9 million compares with a US$15.4 million loss in Q3 2025 and US$17.1 million in Q4 2024, even though quarterly revenue in Q4 2025 was US$13.0 million versus US$12.8 million in Q3 2025 and US$11.9 million in Q4 2024.
  • The balanced consensus view is that Anaphylm and other pipeline assets could eventually support better margins, yet this latest step up in quarterly losses means
    • the current earnings picture still lines up more closely with the caution that analysts build into their three year profitability assumptions, and
    • investors have to compare the consensus revenue growth expectations with a history where quarterly losses can still move sharply even when the top line is relatively stable.

data.title: Tecsys Q3 2026 Net Margin Rebound Tests Premium P/E Narrative
data.description:
Tecsys (TSX:TCS) has put up another solid quarter, reporting Q3 2026 revenue of CA$48.5 million and basic EPS of CA$0.12, alongside trailing twelve month revenue of CA$189.7 million and EPS of about CA$0.40 that reflect the recent rebound in earnings. The company has seen revenue move from CA$45.2 million in Q3 2025 to CA$48.5 million in Q3 2026, while quarterly EPS has shifted from CA$0.08 to CA$0.12 over the same period. This sets up a story of improving profitability that now sits against a trailing net margin of 3.1% versus 1.7% a year ago.

See our full analysis for Tecsys.

With the latest numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this margin profile and earnings rebound line up with the bigger narratives that investors and the market have been building around Tecsys.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

TTM earnings almost double despite soft five year trend

  • Over the last twelve months, Tecsys earned CA$5.97 million of net income and CA$0.40 in basic EPS, compared with a five year record where earnings declined at an average rate of 8.6% per year but rose 98.5% over just the last year.
  • What stands out for a bullish view is how the recent 98.5% earnings improvement contrasts with that 8.6% annual decline over five years. This raises questions about how durable the rebound is when you look at:
    • Trailing twelve month revenue stepping up from CA$172.50 million to CA$189.65 million over the data provided, alongside the lift in EPS from CA$0.17 to CA$0.40.
    • Net profit margin running at 3.1% now versus 1.7% a year earlier, so the current profitability is higher than the longer term earnings decline might imply.

After such a sharp swing in earnings, it is worth seeing how other investors connect these numbers to their longer term Tecsys story, which you can do through the 📊 Read the what the Community is saying about Tecsys.

Margins improve to 3.1% as quarterly income steadies

  • Net profit margin over the trailing year is 3.1% compared with 1.7% a year earlier, and quarterly net income in Q3 2026 sits at CA$1.73 million, close to CA$1.77 million in Q2 2026 and CA$1.71 million in Q4 2025.
  • Critics highlight the multi year earnings decline of 8.6% per year, yet the margin shift and steadier recent net income create a different picture when you line up the figures:
    • Q3 2026 revenue of CA$48.50 million is in the same range as Q2 2026 at CA$48.64 million and Q4 2025 at CA$46.56 million, while EPS across those quarters stays around CA$0.12, suggesting the current earnings level is not just from a single spike.
    • The move in trailing net income from CA$2.57 million to CA$5.97 million across the periods provided means the 3.1% margin is being earned on a higher revenue base than a year earlier.

Premium P/E and DCF gap put focus on expectations

  • The shares trade on a 70.8x P/E versus peers at 36.7x and the North American software industry at 28.2x, while the DCF fair value of about CA$21.12 sits below the current CA$28.90 share price.
  • Bears argue that paying a premium P/E on top of a price that is above the CA$21.12 DCF fair value is hard to justify given the long run earnings decline, and the numbers give some support to that concern:
    • The stock price of CA$28.90 is above both the DCF fair value and a five year earnings record where profits fell 8.6% per year on average even after the recent 98.5% jump.
    • Forecast revenue growth of about 10.4% per year, which is higher than the 6% Canadian market benchmark, means expectations are relying on that revenue profile to keep supporting the current premium valuation.
